enrichment.short_description: Sales performance management software that turns Salesforce data into scorecards, coaching views, and team competitions.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LevelEleven is sales gamification and performance management software built on Salesforce data, now part of Ascent Cloud.

features.title: Performance scorecards
features.description: Turns sales goals into daily tracking metrics so reps can see progress against targets.

features.title: Coaching views
features.description: Gives managers visibility into rep activity and outcomes to support consistent 1:1 coaching.

features.title: Sales competitions
features.description: Runs leaderboards and recognition-based competitions to drive specific selling behaviors.

features.title: KPI tracking
features.description: Tracks pipeline activity and conversion metrics pulled directly from Salesforce.

features.title: Salesforce-native
features.description: Built to work primarily with Salesforce CRM data, available on the Salesforce AppExchange.

pros: Deep, native integration with Salesforce data
Established product with a large installed base of 1,000+ sales teams
Highly rated on TrustRadius, Salesforce AppExchange, and Capterra
Combines coaching, scorecards, and gamified competitions in one tool
cons: No public pricing; requires a custom quote through Ascent Cloud
Primarily built around Salesforce, limiting value for non-Salesforce shops
Now sold as part of the broader Ascent Cloud suite rather than as a fully standalone product

faqs.question: Is LevelEleven still a standalone product?
faqs.answer: LevelEleven is now offered as one of the products within the Ascent Cloud suite, alongside Territory Planner and Geopointe, though it is still marketed under the LevelEleven name.

faqs.question: What CRM does LevelEleven work with?
faqs.answer: LevelEleven is built to work primarily with Salesforce CRM data.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: LevelEleven is available as a free trial through the Salesforce AppExchange.

faqs.question: How is LevelEleven priced?
faqs.answer: Pricing is not published; Ascent Cloud provides custom quotes based on team size and which products (LevelEleven, Territory Planner, Geopointe, Insights) you select.

use_cases.title: Sales coaching cadence
use_cases.description: Managers use scorecards and activity visibility to run consistent coaching conversations with reps.

use_cases.title: Driving specific behaviors
use_cases.description: Teams run time-boxed competitions and leaderboards to push activities like outbound calls or demos scheduled.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LevelEleven?
paragraphs: LevelEleven is sales performance management software that converts Salesforce activity data into scorecards, coaching dashboards, and gamified competitions.
The product is now part of Ascent Cloud, where it operates alongside Territory Planner and Geopointe under a 'Plan-Execute-Grow' framework, but continues to be marketed under the LevelEleven name.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LevelEleven is for
paragraphs: LevelEleven suits Salesforce-based sales organizations that want to translate pipeline and activity data into daily coaching routines and team competitions, rather than teams looking for a CRM-agnostic tool.

enrichment.short_description: Revenue orchestration platform combining AI revenue agents, forecasting, and pipeline inspection for enterprise sales teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Clari is an AI-powered revenue orchestration platform used by 1,500+ companies for forecasting, pipeline inspection, and deal intelligence.

features.title: Revenue AI Agents
features.description: Automated agents that monitor deals, flag risk, and support forecasting across the sales cycle.

features.title: Forecast
features.description: Forecasting and pipeline management tools built to give revenue leaders predictable, up-to-date projections.

features.title: Inspect
features.description: Opportunity management that surfaces deal risk and data quality issues directly from CRM activity.

features.title: Copilot
features.description: Conversation intelligence and coaching drawn from recorded sales calls.

features.title: Groove
features.description: Sales engagement and prospecting workflows built into the platform.

features.title: Revenue Database (RevDB)
features.description: A unified data layer that captures revenue activity across sales, marketing, and post-sales.

pros: Used by 1,500+ customers managing a reported $5 trillion in revenue
Named a 2025 G2 Best Sales Software Winner and a Leader in Gartner's Magic Quadrant for Revenue Action Orchestration
Combines forecasting, conversation intelligence, and engagement in one platform rather than separate point tools
Built for enterprise, cross-functional revenue teams (sales, RevOps, marketing, finance)
cons: No public pricing; requires a custom quote
Positioned for large/enterprise organizations, which may be more than smaller teams need
Founding year and headquarters are not published on the site

faqs.question: How much does Clari cost?
faqs.answer: Clari does not publish pricing; prospective customers must request a quote.

faqs.question: Who uses Clari?
faqs.answer: Clari is used by revenue operations, sales, marketing, post-sales, and finance teams, primarily at larger and enterprise organizations.

faqs.question: Does Clari include conversation intelligence?
faqs.answer: Yes, Clari Copilot provides conversation intelligence and coaching based on recorded sales calls.

faqs.question: Does Clari do sales forecasting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Clari Forecast is a dedicated module for pipeline and revenue forecasting.

use_cases.title: Revenue forecasting
use_cases.description: RevOps and sales leadership use Clari Forecast to build and track forecasts against pipeline data.

use_cases.title: Deal inspection
use_cases.description: Managers use Clari Inspect and Revenue AI Agents to spot at-risk deals before they slip.

use_cases.title: Call coaching
use_cases.description: Teams use Clari Copilot to review recorded calls and coach reps on messaging.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Clari?
paragraphs: Clari is a revenue orchestration platform that pairs AI-driven revenue agents with forecasting, opportunity inspection, conversation intelligence, and sales engagement tools.
The platform is built around a unified Revenue Database that pulls activity from CRM, calls, and other revenue tools so agents can monitor deals across every stage.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Clari is for
paragraphs: Clari targets larger and enterprise revenue organizations that need cross-functional visibility across sales, RevOps, marketing, finance, and post-sales, rather than single-rep or very small-team use.

name: BoostUp
skip_reason: BoostUp.ai now redirects (301) to terret.ai and the site is exclusively branded as 'Terret' / 'Terret Nexus'; the BoostUp name is only visible in the legacy app.boostup.ai login URL, indicating a full rebrand rather than the same product name being retained.

enrichment.short_description: AI revenue platform combining forecasting, conversation intelligence, and deal guidance agents for go-to-market teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Aviso is an AI revenue platform offering forecasting, conversation intelligence, and no-code AI agents for revenue teams.

features.title: Real-time AI avatars
features.description: Role-specific AI agents that provide deal guidance and prospect engagement support.

features.title: No-code Agent Studio
features.description: Lets teams build and deploy custom AI agents for revenue workflows without engineering help.

features.title: Conversation intelligence
features.description: Analyzes sales calls to surface seller performance insights.

features.title: Relationship intelligence
features.description: Tracks multi-threaded stakeholder relationships within deals.

features.title: Revenue forecasting
features.description: Advanced forecasting models for pipeline and revenue projections.

features.title: Pipeline inspection
features.description: Analyzes early-stage and mature pipeline to flag deals needing acceleration.

features.title: Mobile app
features.description: Gives reps and managers an AI-powered revenue view from a mobile device.

pros: Combines forecasting, conversation intelligence, and agent-building tools in one platform
Reports being used by 450+ revenue teams
No-code Agent Studio allows non-technical teams to build custom AI workflows
Offers 30+ pre-built GTM workflows and 13-week cadences
cons: No public pricing; requires a custom proposal
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published on the site
Reported performance statistics (e.g., win-rate increases) are self-reported case study figures, not independently verified

faqs.question: How much does Aviso cost?
faqs.answer: Aviso does not publish pricing; you request a tailored proposal through its site.

faqs.question: Does Aviso offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: This is not stated on the site; pricing and access are handled via a custom proposal process.

faqs.question: Can I build custom AI agents in Aviso?
faqs.answer: Yes, Aviso's no-code Agent Studio lets teams build and deploy AI agents without engineering resources.

faqs.question: Does Aviso include conversation intelligence?
faqs.answer: Yes, Aviso includes conversation intelligence for analyzing sales calls and seller performance.

use_cases.title: Revenue forecasting
use_cases.description: Revenue leaders use Aviso's forecasting models to build more accurate pipeline projections.

use_cases.title: Deal acceleration
use_cases.description: Sales managers use pipeline inspection to identify at-risk deals early and mature-stage deals needing a push.

use_cases.title: Custom AI workflows
use_cases.description: RevOps teams build custom agents in Agent Studio for tasks specific to their sales process.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Aviso?
paragraphs: Aviso is an AI revenue platform that combines forecasting, conversation intelligence, relationship intelligence, and a no-code agent builder for go-to-market teams.
The platform includes role-specific AI agents intended to guide deals and engage prospects, alongside pre-built workflows and cadences for common revenue processes.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Aviso is for
paragraphs: Aviso is aimed at revenue teams (BDRs, SDRs, account executives, and C-suite revenue leaders) at mid-market and enterprise companies that want AI-driven forecasting and deal guidance beyond a single point tool.

name: People.ai
skip_reason: People.ai has been rebranded to 'Backstory'; the redirect target explicitly confirms in its FAQ: 'Is Backstory the same as People.ai? Yes. People.ai is now Backstory.' The product no longer operates under the People.ai name.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered revenue platform that captures sales calls and customer interactions to power forecasting, coaching, and deal insights.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Gong is an AI revenue platform used by 5,000+ companies for call recording, forecasting, sales enablement, and deal intelligence.

features.title: Gong Engage
features.description: AI-driven sales engagement and outreach personalization.

features.title: Gong Forecast
features.description: Revenue forecasting powered by predictive analytics drawn from deal and call data.

features.title: Gong Enable
features.description: AI-powered sales enablement and training built on real call data.

features.title: Gong Agents
features.description: Automated agents that handle tasks like follow-ups, pipeline updates, and enablement workflows.

features.title: Revenue Graph
features.description: A unified data network that captures customer interactions across the business.

features.title: Gong Collective
features.description: An ecosystem of 400+ integrations connecting Gong to other revenue tools.

pros: Rated 4.9/5 across more than 6,200 G2 reviews
Used by 5,000+ customers, including large enterprise accounts
Recognized as a Forrester Wave leader
Broad integration ecosystem (400+ integrations via Gong Collective)
cons: No public pricing; licensing includes a platform fee plus per-user pricing, quoted individually
Aimed at organizations large enough to need forecasting, enablement, and engagement in one platform, which can be more than very small teams need
Founding year and employee count are not published on the site

faqs.question: How is Gong priced?
faqs.answer: Gong prices licenses per user plus a platform fee based on the number of users supported; exact pricing requires a custom quote.

faqs.question: What does Gong do?
faqs.answer: Gong captures customer interactions like calls and meetings, analyzes them with AI, and turns the insights into forecasting, coaching, and workflow automation.

faqs.question: Does Gong offer sales forecasting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Gong Forecast provides revenue forecasting based on predictive analytics.

faqs.question: How many customers does Gong have?
faqs.answer: Gong reports more than 5,000 customers, including Fortune 10 companies.

use_cases.title: Conversation intelligence
use_cases.description: Sales teams record and analyze calls to understand what messaging and behaviors close deals.

use_cases.title: Revenue forecasting
use_cases.description: RevOps and sales leadership use Gong Forecast to build predictive, data-backed forecasts.

use_cases.title: Sales enablement
use_cases.description: Enablement teams use Gong Enable to build training programs grounded in real call data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Gong?
paragraphs: Gong is an AI-powered revenue platform that captures customer interactions such as calls, meetings, and emails, then analyzes them to surface forecasting, coaching, and deal insights.
The platform includes dedicated modules for engagement (Gong Engage), forecasting (Gong Forecast), and enablement (Gong Enable), tied together by a shared Revenue Graph and automated agents.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Gong is for
paragraphs: Gong serves revenue leadership, sales teams and managers, RevOps, customer success, and sales enablement functions, primarily at mid-market and enterprise organizations across industries like technology, financial services, healthcare, and manufacturing.

enrichment.short_description: Conversation intelligence software that records and analyzes sales calls, now sold as Chorus by ZoomInfo.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Chorus (formerly Chorus.ai) is conversation intelligence software from ZoomInfo that records and analyzes sales calls for coaching and deal insight.

features.title: Call transcription and analysis
features.description: Automatically transcribes and analyzes customer calls, meetings, and emails.

features.title: Sales coaching
features.description: Tracks team performance and surfaces coaching opportunities from recorded conversations.

features.title: Deal intelligence
features.description: Analyzes relationship and deal dynamics to give reps and managers visibility into deal health.

features.title: Competitive win/loss analysis
features.description: Identifies competitor mentions and patterns across calls to inform win/loss analysis.

features.title: CRM and Zoom integration
features.description: Automatically connects to Zoom and syncs contacts and communications to the CRM.

features.title: ZoomInfo data integration
features.description: Connects conversation data with ZoomInfo's B2B contact and company database.

pros: Backed by 14 technology patents in machine-learning-based conversation analysis
Won G2 awards for 'Easiest Admin - SMB' and 'Best Results - MM'
Direct integration with ZoomInfo's contact and company database
Automatic Zoom call capture with no manual setup
cons: Now sold only as part of ZoomInfo's platform (Chorus by ZoomInfo) rather than as an independent product
No public pricing; requires a demo or free-trial request to get a quote
Founding year and headquarters are not published on the current product page

faqs.question: Is Chorus.ai still available as a standalone product?
faqs.answer: Chorus.ai was acquired by ZoomInfo and is now sold as 'Chorus' (Chorus by ZoomInfo), integrated with ZoomInfo's broader platform.

faqs.question: What does Chorus do?
faqs.answer: Chorus records, transcribes, and analyzes sales calls and meetings to provide coaching insights, deal intelligence, and win/loss analysis.

faqs.question: Does Chorus integrate with Zoom?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chorus automatically connects to Zoom to capture calls.

faqs.question: How is Chorus priced?
faqs.answer: Pricing is not published; the site directs users to request a demo or free trial for a quote.

use_cases.title: Call coaching
use_cases.description: Sales managers review transcribed and scored calls to coach reps on messaging and objection handling.

use_cases.title: Deal risk visibility
use_cases.description: Reps and managers use deal intelligence to track relationship health and competitive mentions within a deal.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Chorus.ai?
paragraphs: Chorus.ai is conversation intelligence software that records, transcribes, and analyzes customer calls, meetings, and emails to give sales teams visibility into deals and coaching opportunities.
The product was acquired by ZoomInfo and is now marketed as Chorus by ZoomInfo, integrated with ZoomInfo's contact and company database and workflow automation tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Chorus.ai is for
paragraphs: Chorus targets sales teams, revenue enablement managers, and coaching functions at enterprise and mid-market organizations that already use or plan to use ZoomInfo's broader data platform.

enrichment.short_description: AI meeting assistant and revenue intelligence platform offering transcription, coaching, scheduling, and pipeline insights.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Avoma is an AI meeting assistant with transcription, coaching, and revenue intelligence, starting at $19/user/month billed annually.

features.title: AI Meeting Assistant
features.description: Auto-records and transcribes meetings in real time, supporting 40+ languages, and generates AI notes and summaries.

features.title: Scheduler & Lead Router
features.description: Automates meeting scheduling and routes inbound leads to the right rep.

features.title: Conversation intelligence
features.description: Scores calls and analyzes talk patterns to power personalized coaching.

features.title: Revenue intelligence
features.description: Surfaces deal risk alerts, pipeline visibility, win-loss analysis, and churn prediction.

features.title: Ask Avoma
features.description: An AI copilot that answers questions about recorded conversations and deal history.

features.title: Free view-only seats
features.description: Lets collaborators view meeting notes and recordings without a full paid seat.

pros: Transparent published pricing starting at $19/user/month
14-day free trial with no credit card required
Supports transcription in 40+ languages
Over 1,000 integrations, including major CRMs, conferencing tools, and dialers
Available on web with iOS and Android mobile apps
cons: Full conversation intelligence requires the higher $29/user/month tier
Company size is not published
Performance claims like '40% win rate increase' are self-reported

faqs.question: How much does Avoma cost?
faqs.answer: Avoma's AI Meeting Assistant starts at $19/user/month billed annually; adding Conversation Intelligence brings it to $29/user/month billed annually.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Avoma offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Avoma have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Avoma offers free view-only seats for collaborators who need to view notes and recordings without a full paid seat.

faqs.question: What CRMs does Avoma integrate with?
faqs.answer: Avoma integrates with CRMs including Salesforce, HubSpot, and Pipedrive, among 1,000+ total integrations.

faqs.question: When was Avoma founded?
faqs.answer: Avoma was founded in late 2017 in Palo Alto, CA.

use_cases.title: Meeting notes and follow-up
use_cases.description: Teams use Avoma to auto-record, transcribe, and summarize customer meetings so reps spend less time on manual notes.

use_cases.title: Call coaching
use_cases.description: Managers use call scoring and talk-pattern analysis to coach reps on their calls.

use_cases.title: Pipeline and deal risk visibility
use_cases.description: RevOps uses Avoma's revenue intelligence to flag at-risk deals and analyze win/loss patterns.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Avoma?
paragraphs: Avoma is an AI meeting assistant and revenue intelligence platform that automates note-taking, scheduling, and coaching for sales and customer success teams.
Founded in late 2017 in Palo Alto, California, Avoma combines meeting transcription with conversation intelligence and pipeline-level revenue intelligence in a single subscription.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Avoma is for
paragraphs: Avoma is built for sales leaders, RevOps, sales enablement, account executives, and customer success leaders at organizations ranging from SMBs to enterprises that want meeting transcription and coaching without stitching together separate tools.

enrichment.short_description: Conversation and revenue intelligence platform that records calls, emails, and video meetings and syncs AI-extracted insights to the CRM.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Jiminny is a conversation and revenue intelligence platform offering call recording, AI insights, and CRM sync, with a 14-day free trial.

features.title: AI call recording and transcription
features.description: Records and transcribes phone calls, video meetings, and emails automatically.

features.title: Automated CRM logging
features.description: Syncs extracted insights, notes, and activity data directly to the CRM.

features.title: Sales coaching insights
features.description: Surfaces coaching opportunities and performance analysis from recorded conversations.

features.title: Competitive intelligence tracking
features.description: Flags competitor mentions across calls to support competitive analysis.

features.title: Pipeline risk detection
features.description: Analyzes deal conversations to identify pipeline and deal risk.

features.title: AI notetaker
features.description: Automatically captures notes during video and phone conversations.

pros: Rated 4.6 out of 5 by users
14-day free trial with full feature access and no credit card required
SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR, CCPA, and HIPAA compliant
Hosted on AWS with a 99.9% uptime guarantee
Integrates with CRM and communication tools including HubSpot and Aircall
cons: No public pricing tiers; exact plan costs are not disclosed on the site
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not published
Reported case-study results (e.g., ARR growth, churn reduction) are customer-specific, not guaranteed outcomes

faqs.question: Does Jiminny offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jiminny offers a 14-day free trial with full feature access and no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does Jiminny cost?
faqs.answer: Specific pricing tiers are not disclosed on the website; you need to sign up or contact Jiminny for pricing.

faqs.question: Is Jiminny compliant with data regulations?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jiminny is SOC 2 Type II certified and compliant with GDPR, CCPA, and HIPAA.

faqs.question: What does Jiminny integrate with?
faqs.answer: Jiminny integrates with CRM and communication tools including HubSpot and Aircall, among others.

use_cases.title: Automated CRM updates
use_cases.description: Reps use Jiminny to automatically log call and meeting data to the CRM, reducing manual admin work.

use_cases.title: Call coaching
use_cases.description: Managers review AI-analyzed calls to coach reps and track performance trends.

use_cases.title: Deal risk tracking
use_cases.description: RevOps teams use pipeline risk detection to flag deals that need attention.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Jiminny?
paragraphs: Jiminny is a conversation and revenue intelligence platform that captures phone calls, video meetings, and emails, then uses AI to extract insights and sync them automatically to the CRM.
The platform focuses on reducing sales admin work while giving managers visibility into deal progress, competitive mentions, and coaching opportunities.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Jiminny is for
paragraphs: Jiminny is aimed at sales reps, sales managers, and revenue operations teams that want automated call capture and CRM logging without manual note-taking.

enrichment.short_description: Revenue orchestration platform combining sales engagement cadences, conversation intelligence, and AI agents for go-to-market teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Salesloft is a revenue orchestration platform used by 4,000+ companies for sales cadences, forecasting, and conversation intelligence.

features.title: Cadence automation
features.description: Builds and nurtures sales pipeline through automated multi-step outreach sequences.

features.title: Rhythm
features.description: An action recommendation engine that prioritizes what reps should do next.

features.title: Conversation intelligence
features.description: Surfaces insights about buyer and seller behavior from recorded sales conversations.

features.title: Deals management
features.description: Tracks and manages sales opportunities through to close.

features.title: Sales forecasting
features.description: Predictive revenue forecasting built on pipeline and activity data.

features.title: Chat agents
features.description: AI agents that convert website visitors into pipeline.

features.title: AI agents for email
features.description: Automates email workflows and other repetitive outreach tasks.

pros: Used by 4,000+ customers, including IBM, Stripe, Siemens, and 3M
Combines sales engagement, forecasting, and conversation intelligence in one platform
Has an integrations marketplace for connecting additional tools
Established headquarters and multiple offices (Atlanta, London, Mexico)
cons: No public pricing; requires contacting sales for a quote
Founding year and company size are not published on the site
Case study results (e.g., '3M closed deals 2.5x faster') are customer-specific outcomes, not guarantees

faqs.question: How much does Salesloft cost?
faqs.answer: Salesloft does not publish pricing; you need to contact sales for a quote.

faqs.question: Where is Salesloft headquartered?
faqs.answer: Salesloft is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, with additional offices in London and Mexico.

faqs.question: Does Salesloft include conversation intelligence?
faqs.answer: Yes, Salesloft includes conversation intelligence that surfaces insights about buyer and seller behavior from sales calls.

faqs.question: Does Salesloft have an integrations marketplace?
faqs.answer: Yes, Salesloft has an integrations marketplace at marketplace.salesloft.com for connecting third-party tools.

use_cases.title: Outbound sales cadences
use_cases.description: SDRs and AEs use cadence automation to run consistent, multi-step outreach sequences.
use_cases.audience: Sales development reps and account executives

use_cases.title: Deal and pipeline management
use_cases.description: Sales teams track opportunities and get next-best-action guidance through Rhythm.

use_cases.title: Website visitor conversion
use_cases.description: Marketing and sales teams use Chat Agents to convert website visitors into pipeline.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Salesloft?
paragraphs: Salesloft is a revenue orchestration platform that connects revenue data with AI agents to help go-to-market teams prioritize actions from first contact through renewal and expansion.
The platform combines cadence automation, an action recommendation engine (Rhythm), conversation intelligence, deal management, and forecasting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Salesloft is for
paragraphs: Salesloft is built for sales leaders, RevOps teams, account executives, SDRs, and customer success managers, and is used by enterprise companies including IBM, Stripe, and 3M.
